CCS356 Object Oriented Software Engineering Lab - Simplified Exam Guide for all 12 questions

CCS356 — Object Oriented Software Engineering Lab

Simplified exam reference • All 12 questions • Diagrams: Use Case, Class, Sequence, Activity, Package, Deployment

Q1Passport Automation System
Applicant applies online → system verifies details → passport dispatched. Actors: Applicant, Admin. Use cases: Registration, Check Status, Process Application, Dispatch Passport.
Q2Book Bank System
Members borrow/reserve books. Librarian manages stock. Actors: Member, Librarian. Use cases: Issue Book, Return Book, Reserve Book, Add/Remove Titles.
Q3Exam Registration System
Students register for exams online and pay fees. Actors: Student, Exam Controller. Use cases: Login, View Exam Details, Pay Fees, Display Results, Logout.
Q4Stock Maintenance System
Tracks product inventory, orders and shipments. Actors: Customer, Salesperson. Use cases: Place Order, Track Order, Validate Customer, Ship Order, Bill Customer.
Q5Online Course Reservation System
Students enroll in courses and get certificates. Actors: Student, Professor, Admin. Use cases: Browse Courses, Enroll, View Schedule, Issue Certificate.
Q6Airline / Railway Reservation System
Passengers book, cancel and check tickets online. Actors: Passenger, Admin. Use cases: Login, Search, Book Ticket, Cancel, Check Status, Logout.
Q7Software Personnel Management (HR)
Manages employee hiring, payroll, attendance and training. Actors: HR Manager, Employee. Use cases: Recruit, Manage Payroll, Track Attendance, Assign Training.
Q8Credit Card Processing System
Processes payments from customer to merchant via card network. Actors: Cashier, Customer, Authorization Service. Use cases: Enter Card, Authorize Payment, Approve/Decline, Generate Receipt.
Q9E-Book Management System
Digital lending platform — members borrow/reserve e-books. Actors: Member, Librarian. Use cases: Login, Browse, Borrow E-Book, Reserve, Return, Notify.
Q10Recruitment System
Job seekers register; employers shortlist candidates. Actors: Job Seeker, Employer, Admin. Use cases: Register, Upload Resume, Search Jobs, Shortlist, Notify.
Q11Foreign Trading System
Traders buy/sell commodities across borders. Actors: Trader, Admin, Database. Use cases: Login, Browse Commodities, Place Order, Pay, Get Bill, Logout.
Q12Conference Management System
Candidates submit research papers; reviewers select best ones. Actors: Candidate, Reviewer. Use cases: Login, Submit Paper, Review Paper, Send Acknowledgement, Register.
Q1

Passport Automation System

What it does
An online system where citizens apply for a passport. The system verifies documents, processes the application, and dispatches the passport — replacing manual paperwork.
Actors
Applicant
Registers, checks status
Administrator
Verifies, processes, dispatches
Use cases
  • Registration (fill personal details)
  • Enter Applicant ID
  • Check Application Status
  • Process Application
  • Dispatch Passport
Key classes
NewRegistration
Gender
ApplicationStatus
AdminAuthentication
AdminPanel
Diagram summary
Use Case
Applicant → Register, CheckStatus; Admin → Process, Dispatch
Activity
Fill form → verify → process → dispatch
Sequence
Applicant→System: submit form; System→DB: validate; DB→System: ok; System→Applicant: ID
Class
5 classes with attributes and operations
Package
UI package, Business Logic, Database
Deployment
Client → Web Server (Glassfish) → Oracle DB
Exam tip: The sequence diagram has 3 flows — Register, Admin processing, and Check Status.
Q2

Book Bank System

What it does
A library system for lending and managing books/magazines. Members can borrow, return, and reserve titles. The librarian manages inventory, adds new books and removes old ones.
Actors
Member/Student
Borrows, returns, reserves books
Librarian
Manages stock, notifies members
Use cases
  • Login with PIN
  • Search & Browse Books
  • Issue / Borrow Book
  • Return Book
  • Reserve Book (if unavailable)
  • Pay Online / Renew
  • Add / Remove Titles (Librarian)
Key classes
Member
Book
Loan
Reservation
Librarian
Diagram summary
Use Case
Member → Issue/Return/Reserve; Librarian → Add/Remove
Activity
Enter PIN → validate → issue or return → update DB
Sequence
Member→System: request book; System→DB: check; DB→System: available; System: issue
Deployment
Client → Glassfish Server → Oracle DB
Exam tip: Sequence diagram shows deposit process — enter PIN → validate → issue/return → pay/renew.
Q3

Exam Registration System

What it does
Students register for exams online, view exam/fee details, and pay fees. The exam controller manages and displays exam information.
Actors
Student
Registers, pays fees, views exams
Exam Controller
Manages registrations
Use cases
  • Login
  • View Exam Details
  • View Fee Details
  • Pay Fee
  • Display Details / Results
  • Logout
Key classes
Student
ExamDetails
FeeDetails
Registration
Controller
Diagram summary
Use Case
Student: Login, ViewExam, PayFee, Logout
Activity
Login → select exam → view fee → pay → confirm
Sequence
Student→UI: login; UI→DB: verify; DB: ok; Student: fill form; System: confirm
Deployment
Browser → Glassfish Server → SQL DB
Exam tip: Very similar to Passport system. The actors are Student and Controller instead of Applicant and Admin.
Q4

Stock Maintenance System

What it does
Tracks company inventory, manages customer orders, and handles shipping. The salesperson manages stock updates and billing. Customers place and track orders.
Actors
Customer
Places and tracks orders
Salesperson
Updates stock, ships, bills
Use cases
  • Place Order
  • Track Order
  • Validate Customer
  • Ship Order (Full or Partial)
  • Bill Customer
Key classes
Customer
Order
Item
Salesperson
Invoice
Diagram summary
Use Case
Customer→PlaceOrder, TrackOrder; Salesperson→ShipOrder, BillCustomer
Activity
Order placed → validate → check stock → ship → bill
Sequence
Customer→System: order; System→DB: check stock; System→Customer: confirm; Salesperson: ships
Deployment
Client → Eclipse/App Server → Database
Exam tip: "Ship Partial Order" is a key use case — include it! It means the system can ship available items even if the full order isn't ready.
Q5

Online Course Reservation System

What it does
Students browse available courses, enroll, and receive certificates on completion. Professors are assigned to courses. Admin manages the course catalog.
Actors
Student
Enroll, view schedule
Professor
Teaches courses
Admin
Manages course list
Use cases
  • Login
  • Browse / View Available Courses
  • Enroll in Course
  • View Schedule
  • Complete Course
  • Receive Certificate
Key classes
Student
Course
Professor
Enrollment
Certificate
Diagram summary
Use Case
Student: Browse→Enroll→Complete→Certificate
Activity
Login → view courses → select → enroll → attend → get certificate
Sequence
Student requests enrollment; System checks seats; confirms; adds to course
Deployment
Browser → App Server → DB
Exam tip: The system must "maintain a list of professors teaching each course" — include Professor as a class with teachCourse() method.
Q6

Airline / Railway Reservation System

What it does
Passengers search for flights/trains, book tickets, cancel bookings, and check travel status online. Admin manages schedules and availability.
Actors
Passenger
Books, cancels tickets
Admin
Manages schedules
Use cases
  • Login
  • Search Flight/Train
  • View Availability
  • Book Ticket
  • Cancel Booking
  • Check Status
  • Logout
Key classes
Passenger
Ticket
Flight/Train
Booking
Admin
Diagram summary
Use Case
Passenger: Search→Book→Cancel; Admin: ManageSchedule
Activity
Login → search → select seat → pay → confirm/cancel
Sequence
User→UI: search; UI→DB: check seats; DB: available; User: select; System: book
Deployment
Client Browser → Web Server → Booking DB
Exam tip: This is an E-Ticketing system. The lab manual covers it under Ex.7. The main flow is: login → search → fill personal details → select seat → book → confirm.
Q7

Software Personnel Management System (HR)

What it does
Manages all HR functions in an organization — hiring, payroll, attendance, training, and performance tracking. Aligns HR operations with financial and security systems.
Actors
HR Manager
Recruits, manages payroll
Employee
Receives salary, attends training
Use cases
  • Recruit Employee
  • Manage Payroll / Salary
  • Track Attendance
  • Assign Training
  • Monitor Performance
  • Manage Time (for tasks)
Key classes
Employee
HRManager
Payroll
Training
Attendance
Diagram summary
Use Case
HR: Recruit, Payroll, Training; Employee: Attendance, Performance
Activity
Post job → interview → hire → onboard → assign tasks → pay salary
Sequence
HR→System: add employee; System→DB: store; System→Employee: notify; HR: process payroll
Deployment
Client → Glassfish/SQL Server → SQL DB
Exam tip: Activity diagram uses fork/join notation — payroll, attendance, training happen in parallel.
Q8

Credit Card Processing System

What it does
Processes credit card payments. The cashier enters card details; the authorization service validates and approves or declines. The merchant receives the result and records the transaction.
Actors
Cashier
Enters card, processes payment
User/Customer
Makes payment
Auth Service
Validates card, approves/declines
Use cases
  • Enter Card Details
  • Send for Authorization
  • Approve Payment
  • Decline Payment
  • Save Order / Transaction
  • Show Confirmation
Key classes
Cashier
User
AuthorizationService
Diagram summary
Use Case
Cashier: EnterCard, ProcessPayment; AuthService: Validate, Approve/Decline
Activity
Enter card → send to network → check limit → approve/decline → receipt
Sequence
Cashier→System: card details; System→AuthService: authorize; AuthService: approve; System: receipt
Deployment
POS Terminal → Payment Gateway → Bank Server
Exam tip: Only 3 classes needed — Cashier, User, AuthorizationService. Architecture: UI (Swing) → Business Logic → Database layer.
Q9

E-Book Management System

What it does
Digital version of a book bank. Members borrow/reserve e-books online. Librarian manages titles and notifies members when reserved books are available.
Actors
Member
Borrows, reserves e-books
Librarian
Manages titles, notifies
Use cases
  • Login / Register
  • Browse E-Books
  • Borrow E-Book
  • Reserve E-Book
  • Return E-Book
  • Receive Notification
  • Add / Remove Titles (Librarian)
Key classes
Member
EBook
Loan
Reservation
Librarian
Diagram summary
Use Case
Same as Book Bank but for e-books
Activity
Login → search → borrow/reserve → download → return
Sequence
Member requests e-book; System checks DB; if available: grant access; else: reserve
Deployment
Client → Glassfish (Oracle) → Oracle DB
Exam tip: Very similar to Book Bank (Q2) — same actors and almost same use cases. Key difference: digital access, no physical dispatch.
Q10

Recruitment System

What it does
Online job portal where job seekers register and employers find candidates. The system reduces queue time for registration and speeds up the hiring process.
Actors
Job Seeker
Registers, uploads resume
Employer
Searches, shortlists
Admin
Manages portal
Use cases
  • Register (get ID)
  • Pay Enrollment Fee
  • Upload Resume
  • Search Candidates (Employer)
  • Shortlist Candidate
  • Notify Candidate
  • Check Application Status
Key classes
JobSeeker
Employer
Application
Admin
Notification
Diagram summary
Use Case
JobSeeker: Register, Upload; Employer: Search, Shortlist
Activity
Register → pay fee → get ID → profile visible → employer shortlists
Sequence
Register flow + Status check flow (2 separate diagrams)
Deployment
Browser → SQL App Server → SQL DB
Exam tip: The sequence diagram has two flows — Registration and Check Status. Very similar structure to Passport Automation.
Q11

Foreign Trading System

What it does
International commodity trading platform. Traders browse commodities, place orders, and make payments. Admin verifies availability and generates bills. Handles currency exchange risks.
Actors
Trader
Browse, order, pay
Administrator
Verify, update, bill
Use cases
  • Login (username + password)
  • Browse / Source Commodities
  • Place Order
  • Check Availability (Admin)
  • Pay Amount
  • Generate Bill
  • Logout
Key classes
Trader
Admin
Commodity
Order
Bill
Database
Diagram summary
Use Case
Trader: Login, Browse, Order, Pay; Admin: Verify, Update, Bill
Activity
Login → source commodities → choose → order → admin checks → pay → bill → logout
Sequence
Trader→System: login; System→Admin: authorize; Trader: select commodity; Admin: check DB; Trader: pay
Deployment
Client → Web Server → Trading DB
Exam tip: The admin plays a central verification role after every order. Always show the admin checking availability in the sequence/activity diagrams.
Q12

Conference Management System

What it does
Platform for academic conferences. Candidates submit research papers, reviewers evaluate them, send acknowledgements (accepted/rejected), and selected candidates register for the event.
Actors
Candidate
Submits paper, registers
Reviewer
Reviews, sends acknowledgement
Database
Verifies login, stores details
Use cases
  • Login to System
  • Submit Paper Title
  • Review Paper (Reviewer)
  • Send Acknowledgement (Accept/Reject)
  • Revise & Submit Camera-ready Paper
  • Register for Conference
Key classes
Candidate
Reviewer
Paper
Acknowledgement
Registration
Diagram summary
Use Case
Candidate: Submit, Register; Reviewer: Review, Acknowledge
Activity
Login → submit paper → reviewer reads → accept/reject → revise → register
Sequence
Candidate→System: submit; System→Reviewer: notify; Reviewer→System: decision; System→Candidate: acknowledgement
Deployment
Client → Web Server → Conference DB
Exam tip: Two-stage paper submission — first the title/abstract, then the camera-ready final paper after acceptance. Include both in your use case diagram.
